name: Aircraft Mechanics
description: The understanding of aerodynamics and the training to repair, rebuild, modify, and redesign conventional aircraft; including single engines, twin engine airplanes, jets, helicopters and hovercraft.

name: Automotive Mechanics
description: The ability to repair, rebuild, modify, and redesign conventional vehicles with internal combustion (gas) engines. It also includes body work, turbine engines, methanol, ethanol and diesel truck engines.

situationalModifiers.task: Hover jet systems for ground vehicles

name: Basic Mechanics
description: A general familiarity and understanding of basic mechanics. This character can fix a toaster, repair a bicycle, replace a belt on a motor, repair or replace a switch, handle or a knob, replace a spark plug, change oil, assist in automobile repairs, maintain machinery, read a schematic and similar fundamental tasks.

name: Locksmith

synergies.description: +5% when Electrical Engineer is known instead of Basic Electronics alone (Nightbane RPG).

prerequisites.label: At least Basic Electronics (Electrical Engineer satisfies and grants +5%)

description: The study of lock designs and the ability to repair, build, modify and open locks. The methods, techniques, and tools of lock picking include the old-style key and tumbler, combination, and modern electrical locking systems.
failureResults: The lock is not opened; repeat the attempt.

performanceFormulas.linkSummary: Antiquated key type lock or simple tumbler/combination type.
Simple electronic lock (usually by patching in a bypass system).
Complex, state-of-the-art electronic lock system such as those used in high security and restricted areas.
Super high-tech systems, such as those used by the military and government.

resolutionLogic.summary: If an attempt to open an electronic lock fails, roll again. A second failed roll means the lock is irreparably damaged and cannot be opened.

situationalModifiers.task: Complex or high-tech locks without Electrical Engineer
situationalModifiers.condition: Character has Basic Electronics but not Electrical Engineer

situationalModifiers.task: Super high-tech military or government lock systems

conditionalRelatedSkills.summary: +4% bonus to Safe-Cracking when the character also has Locksmith.

name: Mechanical Engineer

synergies.description: One-time +5% to Locksmith when Mechanical Engineer is also known.
One-time +5% to Surveillance Systems when Mechanical Engineer is also known.

description: Training, understanding, and knowledge of how machinery is designed, operated, built, and maintained. Characters can attempt to redesign, modify, repair, construct, or sabotage mechanical devices (includes nuclear reactor driven turbines and atomic engines).

sequentialChecks.summary: Roll first to figure out how to operate/analyze/design a machine; on success, roll again to fix/change/build the mechanism.
sequentialChecks.condition: When redesigning, modifying, repairing, constructing, or sabotaging mechanical devices.

conditionalRelatedSkills.summary: +6% bonus to Safe-Cracking when the character also has Mechanical Engineer.
+10% bonus to Jury-Rig when the character is a Mechanical Engineer.

name: Vehicle Armorer

synergies.description: +10% bonus to Automotive Mechanics when the character has Vehicle Armorer.

prerequisites.narrativeRequirement: Usually reserved for bounty hunters, mercenaries, mechanical specialists working for criminal organizations, survivalist or anti-Nightlord groups, military contractors, or military personnel. Most modifications are illegal outside the military or authorized agencies in industrialized nations.

description: A specialized skill in which the character can do more than just tinker with the mechanics of a vehicle. Includes extra armor, ram prow installation, custom body modifications, and replacing components and parts on military and civilian vehicles.

grantsSkills.summary: Automatically grants Basic Mechanics at book base +20% as part of this skill.

mechanicProgressions.summary: Increase the A.R. of pre-existing or added vehicle armor by 1 point per 3 levels of experience.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Extra armor: add 25 S.D.C. per level of experience to military/combat vehicles, full-sized vans, and large trucks; 10 S.D.C. per level on commercial (non-combat) vehicles.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When adding armor to a vehicle.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Ram prow: +2D6 S.D.C. damage on ram attacks, plus +1D6 S.D.C. for every 40 mph (64 km) of speed. Ram prow has 2D4×10 S.D.C. +15 S.D.C. per level of experience.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When a ram prow is installed and used in a ram attack.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Reduce vehicle speed by 5% for every extra 40 S.D.C. of armor on military vehicles, or every 20 S.D.C. added to commercial vehicles. Reduce speed 10% for a ram prow.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When calculating speed for an armored or ram-prow-equipped vehicle.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Custom body modifications: repaint or reconfigure appearance; sealed environmental interior; reinforced pilot/crew compartment (50 S.D.C., +10 S.D.C. per level); relocate engine or fuel tank; extra fuel tank; heavy-duty shock absorbers (+40 S.D.C. to reinforced compartment); extend/shorten wheelbase; secret compartments; and similar changes.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When performing custom body work on a vehicle.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Replace components and parts: handgrips (2D4 S.D.C. each), railings (1D6+4 S.D.C. each), headlights (1D6 S.D.C.), spotlight (20 S.D.C.), searchlight (30–50 S.D.C.), locking hatches (60 S.D.C./sq. ft. +10 S.D.C. per level, A.R. 16), armored glass slits (75 S.D.C. +5 S.D.C. per level, A.R. 14), roof turret (250 S.D.C. +10 S.D.C. per level, A.R. 12), and weapon mounts for heavy vehicle weapons.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When adding or replacing vehicle components.

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Can add or repair weapon mounts for heavy vehicle-mounted weapons but cannot hook up the weapon, ammo drum, or modify the actual weapons without Weapon Systems.
Can also drive or operate basic military vehicles and trucks at the base skill for that vehicle type.
Acquiring military-grade weapons, ammo, parts, and vehicles may be half the adventure (see Find Contraband).

name: Weapons Engineer
description: The character can maintain, repair, modify, mount, and figure out most weapon systems. He can repair an assault rifle and install a missile system into a vehicle. The engineer can also add and repair body armor and is an expert welder.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: This skill is usually reserved for military personnel.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When assigning or qualifying for this skill.
